narrative: Markets open with a notable contradiction at the forefront: news flow is firmly risk-off, driven by escalating US-Iran tensions and a crude oil surge weighing on equities, yet the technical regime reads Risk-On High with the USD ranking as one of the weakest currencies at 43 — undermining the expected safe-haven bid. This divergence deserves caution. AUDUSD stands out as the clearest convergence point, with both Daily and H4 timeframes aligned on Buy signals and AUD topping the currency strength table at 100, suggesting the technical momentum is overriding geopolitical headwinds for now. Meanwhile, JPY crosses like GBPJPY and EURJPY show H4 buy momentum, though Daily neutrality warrants restraint given BoJ's medium-term hawkish pivot could cap upside. GBP faces its own headwind from dovish BoE implications following UK labour slack data. The USD/CAD pair deserves close attention today as simultaneous Fed and BoC decisions create compounding event risk — any hawkish Fed surprise could sharply reprice this cross.

themes.theme: US-Iran geopolitical tensions driving risk aversion and crude oil surge
Fed decision imminent creating USD uncertainty
BoJ hawkish shift supporting JPY medium-term
Risk-off sentiment pressuring commodity and high-beta currencies
Crude oil surge lifting energy-linked commodities but weighing on equities
EUR finding mild support on USD uncertainty despite risk-off
UK labour slack containing inflation, dovish BoE implications
Precious metals under technical pressure despite risk-off backdrop
BoC decision alongside Fed creating event risk for USD/CAD

key_observation: US-Iran tensions are the dominant macro driver this session — stalled talks are lifting crude oil sharply, fueling broad risk aversion in equities and high-beta FX, while safe-haven USD and JPY benefit. The Fed and BoC double-header adds event risk but geopolitics are leading price action.
watch: Any breakthrough or escalation in US-Iran talks will be the primary catalyst; also watch Fed statement tone on tariff-driven inflation vs growth risks, and BoC rate decision for CAD volatility.
